perf_hint: frame time scales with pixel count, not with `steps` — the nine texture samples of the previous frame and the readback dominate, so on software rendering eight reaction sub-steps cost under 10% more than one, not eight times more. Turn `steps` down to slow the ink down, not to speed the render up; preview with `--sample 10s` before committing to 1080p, which is a little over twice the work of 720p

params.name: diffusion
params.description: How far the ink bleeds into the water around it each frame.

params.name: growth
params.description: How hard the ink makes more of itself. Scaled by the loudness of the song.

params.name: dissolve
params.description: How fast the ink gives up. Above about a quarter of the growth rate it wins, and a passage clears the frame.

params.name: crowd
params.description: How much water a neighbour's ink uses up. Above 1.0 a dense blob starves, stops filling, and hollows out.

params.name: steps
params.description: Reaction sub-steps per frame: how far the ink gets in a thirtieth of a second, and how sharp its fronts are.

params.name: seed_rate
params.description: How much new ink the song feeds into the water between hits.

params.name: detail
params.description: How many blooms of new ink span the short edge of the frame.

params.name: swirl
params.description: How hard the bass stirs the water the ink is drifting in.

params.name: flash
params.description: How much ink an onset drops into the middle of the frame.

params.name: hue_cycle
params.description: How fast the palette walks under the ink, in cycles per second.

params.name: vignette
params.description: How far from the walls of the dish the ink stops being able to grow.

params.name: brightness
params.description: How much light the ink gives back. It can never emit more than it covers.
